title: 6.1.1 Food Chain and Food Web
description: Food chains and food webs — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: What is the ultimate source of energy for all living organisms?
What is the best way to trap and store energy from sunlight?
options: Growing plants and using the food they synthesize
questions.correctAnswer: Growing plants and using the food they synthesize

questions.question: Plants that first trap energy and produce their food are called what?
What is the scientific term for producers?
What does the word 'autotroph' mean?
Animals that feed on plants or other animals for energy are known as what?
What is the scientific term for consumers?
What is a herbivore?
options: An animal that feeds on other animals
An animal that feeds on plants
An animal that feeds on both plants and animals
An organism that decomposes dead matter
questions.correctAnswer: An animal that feeds on plants

questions.question: What is a carnivore?
options: An animal that feeds on plants
An animal that feeds on other animals
An animal that feeds on both plants and animals
questions.correctAnswer: An animal that feeds on other animals

questions.question: What is an omnivore?
options: An animal that feeds only on plants
An animal that feeds only on other animals
An animal that feeds on both plants and animals
An organism that feeds on dead matter
questions.correctAnswer: An animal that feeds on both plants and animals

questions.question: The transfer of energy from plants through a chain of organisms is called what?
options: The food chain or food web
questions.correctAnswer: The food chain or food web

questions.question: What process is repeatedly described as 'to eat or to be eaten'?
What does a food chain represent among the biotic components of an ecosystem?
Where are producers located in a food chain?
What type of energy flow occurs from the sun to producers and then to consumers?
An organism that feeds on both plants and animals occupies how many positions in the food chain?
What is a food web?
options: A single straight path of energy flow
A complex web of interconnected food chains
questions.correctAnswer: A complex web of interconnected food chains

questions.question: What does a food web create due to interconnected food chains?
How does energy flow in a food web?
options: Energy does not flow in a food web

questions.question: In ecosystems, which feeding structure is more realistic than a simple chain?
How many paths does a food chain follow?
How many paths does a food web follow?
What is a trophic level?
options: The amount of biomass in an organism
The position of an organism in the order of receiving energy
The speed at which an organism eats
The type of habitat an organism lives in
questions.correctAnswer: The position of an organism in the order of receiving energy

questions.question: Trophic levels usually range from what number to what number?
What organisms stand at the first trophic level?
What organisms stand at the second trophic level?
What organisms stand at the third trophic level?
What is a feeding level?
options: The order of consuming or eating one another
The time of day an organism feeds
questions.correctAnswer: The order of consuming or eating one another

questions.question: At the first feeding level, which organisms consume plants?
At the second feeding level, which organisms eat herbivores?
What is the difference between trophic level and feeding level?
options: Trophic level is about receiving energy, feeding level is about consuming others
Feeding level is about energy, trophic level is about eating
questions.correctAnswer: Trophic level is about receiving energy, feeding level is about consuming others

questions.question: In Figure 6.2, which organism is the secondary consumer and stands at the third trophic level?
In Figure 6.2, which organism is the primary consumer at the first feeding level?
In Figure 6.2, which organism is the producer at the first trophic level?
In Figure 6.1, what does the abbreviation P represent?
In Figure 6.1, what does the abbreviation H represent?
In Figure 6.1, what does C1 represent?
In Figure 6.1, what does C2 represent?
